queue.description: Replace fixed-horizon labels with triple-barrier (profit-taking at +2σ, stop-loss at -1σ, time-out at 100 bars).
queue.hypothesis: Vol bars create more IID returns so triple-barrier thresholds will be more stable.

queue.description: Compute rolling Hurst exponent. H > 0.6 → trend labels. H < 0.45 → MR labels. On vol bars.
queue.hypothesis: Adaptive switching prevents applying wrong label type to wrong regime.

queue.description: Combine labels at multiple forward horizons (50, 100, 200) into consensus. Only trade when all agree.
Top tertile = long, bottom = skip, middle = no-trade. Avoids noisy middle.
queue.hypothesis: Skipping middle tertile increases signal purity.

queue.description: Secondary XGBoost predicts whether primary prediction is correct. Only trade when both agree.
Use CUSUM or PELT on volatility/price for regime boundaries. Label post-change regimes by return.
queue.hypothesis: Change-point detection explicitly models regime shifts.

queue.description: Use range bars (price-move-based sampling) instead of vol/dollar/tick. Uniform price space.
queue.hypothesis: Range bars normalize price movement for volatile ETFs.

queue.description: Replace XGBoost with Ridge + isotonic calibration. Less overfitting for linear signals.
queue.hypothesis: Ridge + isotonic may beat XGBoost on assets with linear signal structure.
